signals.title: GBP/NZD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: GBP/NZD spread at 1.242% marks a strong G10 theme as NZD 1W avg performance hits -0.771% (n=4). NZD COT LF net short -22,454 (long% 18.4%) represents a positioning extreme, yet specs are correctly aligned with the -0.83% negative real rate. Despite RBNZ hike bias, the 'Antipodean slide' headline and risk-off mood (VIX +14.5%) accelerate the divergence. GBP remains the G10 leader (+0.471%) supported by a +0.77% real rate. GBP/NZD at 2.3219.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.39 holds above the 160.00 intervention threshold as +275bp Fed-BoJ carry dominates. VRP at +4.8% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.4%) signals the options market is pricing significant tail risk despite 'normal' VIX 19.8. Retail sentiment is 86% short, diverging from the JPY COT LF net short of -112,092. This institutional-retail divergence at 161+ suggests retail is fading a carry trade that remains fundamentally supported by the +1.48% US real rate vs -0.44% JPY real rate.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + CESI Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation at -0.566 vs -0.010 norm (z=-2.00σ) signals a significant regime break where CAD is failing to track energy prices. USD CESI net +30 (strong positive) vs CAD net +9 confirms US data outperformance is the primary driver. CAD COT LF net short -67,668 (long% 23.4%) is approaching extreme levels while retail is 95% short. The combination of data beats and the 150bp OIS carry advantage supports USD/CAD at 1.4179.

signals.title: EUR/JPY — Options Skew + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/JPY 25d RR at -1.51 (Strong Put Skew) signals the options market is paying a premium for EUR downside protection despite a +125bp carry advantage. EUR COT LF net short -40,372 (long% 39.1%) aligns with this cautious outlook. While Eurozone PMIs (Services 48.9) show stabilization, the negative options skew suggests the market is hedging against a JPY safe-haven bid or carry unwind. Watch for a break of 184.00 to confirm downside momentum.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry Neutrality
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 6.7% generates a +4.3% VRP, the second highest in G10, indicating heavy hedging ahead of the July 30 BoE meeting. With OIS carry neutral (-11bp), price action at 1.3228 is driven by the +0.77% real rate advantage over the USD. Speculative positioning remains net long (+17,711), but the high vol premium suggests traders are not adding to length at these levels. Watch for US 10Y yield shifts to break the 1.32 handle.
